laws.title: Public Law 12-83 | Dannybec Went to Jail Act
laws.final_statutory_text: Direct repeal of Public Law 7-42 and immediate shutdown of the Department of Transportation, with related employee-relief language. No operative U.S. Code text survives.
laws.chronology: Current repeal enactment; no later superseding repeal identified in the reviewed source set.
laws.repeal_transfer_redesignation: Direct repeal only; no transfer or redesignation issue requiring permanent Code text.
laws.rationale: This is a permanent repeal-and-shutdown enactment, but it does not create a clean ongoing Code rule. A concise statutory history note is legally sufficient.

laws.title: Public Law 12-84 | Municipal Act of 2023
laws.final_statutory_text: Reestablishes the District of Columbia municipal government and charter structure, including mayoral, council, police, and fire provisions.
laws.chronology: Current local enactment; no later federal Code placement identified in the reviewed materials.
laws.repeal_transfer_redesignation: Local District governance rewrite only; no federal Code transfer or redesignation is appropriate.
laws.rationale: The act is local/District legislation rather than a permanent federal positive-law rule. Historical-note treatment is enough.

laws.title: Public Law 12-86 | H.R. 1302
laws.final_statutory_text: Repeals title II, section 007 of the New District of Columbia Home Rule Act; no separate Code section is created.
laws.chronology: Current repeal enactment affecting District charter material; no later superseding repeal identified.
laws.repeal_transfer_redesignation: Direct repeal of a local charter provision; no federal transfer or redesignation issue remains.
laws.rationale: This is a local repeal of an already-local charter provision. A history note is legally sufficient.

laws.title: Public Law 12-87 | An act to repeal the Smart Label Congress Act (Public Law 9-54)
laws.final_statutory_text: Express repeal of Public Law 9-54, together with chamber-labeling and archival duties for the repeal record.
laws.chronology: Current repeal enactment; the reviewed materials do not identify a later superseding repeal.
laws.repeal_transfer_redesignation: Complete repeal of the earlier act; no transfer or redesignation survives.
laws.rationale: The act functions as repeal history and administrative recordkeeping. No operative Code section is needed.

laws.title: Public Law 12-88 | New District of Columbia Home Rule Act
laws.final_statutory_text: A broad District of Columbia home-rule rewrite covering mayoral powers, council structure, the attorney general, initiative and recall, and related charter mechanics.
laws.chronology: Current local enactment; no later federal Code placement or permanent positive-law section was safely resolved.
laws.repeal_transfer_redesignation: Local government restructuring and repeal clauses only; no federal Code transfer or redesignation issue remains.
laws.rationale: This is a District-local governance enactment, not a general federal Code rule. Historical-note treatment is sufficient.

laws.title: Public Law 12-103 | Ceremonial 5-Star Officer Creation Act
laws.final_statutory_text: Creates a one-at-a-time ceremonial 5-star/O-11 officer grade with appointment, retirement, and advisory rules in Title 10.
laws.chronology: Current enactment; no later repeal identified in the reviewed materials.
laws.repeal_transfer_redesignation: No repeal or transfer issue; the problem is undercodification at the wrong title.
laws.rationale: This is a permanent military grade rule and belongs in operative Title 10 text. A note under Title 36 is not legally sufficient.

laws.title: Public Law 14-89 | Americans Safety and Smart Act of 2023
laws.final_statutory_text: Creates federal misconduct and force offenses, including unlawful detention or arrest, warrantless seizure, taser use, and public-way interference, and repeals Public Law 2-6.
laws.chronology: Current enactment in the reviewed source set; no later repeal of the core framework was identified.
laws.repeal_transfer_redesignation: Direct repeal of Public Law 2-6 plus new criminal provisions; this needs operative Title 18 treatment rather than history only.
laws.rationale: The act is a substantive, permanent criminal framework. A note-only approach under § 242 does not fully capture the enacted rule set.

laws.title: Public Law 14-90 | Presidential Suck Up Act
laws.final_statutory_text: Allows listed nominees to act while awaiting Senate confirmation and ends that authority on rejection; the ambassador clause was later repealed.
laws.chronology: Partially current; SEC 2(a)(i) for United States ambassadors is expressly repealed by later PL 15-102 SEC II(b), while the remainder stays operative.
laws.repeal_transfer_redesignation: Partial repeal only, limited to the ambassador subclause; the rest of the acting-service rule remains in force.
laws.rationale: The act is operative except for the later-repealed ambassador subclause. A concise statutory note at Title 3 is legally sufficient if it reflects the partial repeal.

laws.title: Public Law 14-91 | Uniform Code of Military Justice Reform Act of 2023
laws.final_statutory_text: Short title, definitions, findings, severability, and a directive to apply a referenced 'Proposed Uniform Code of Military Justice' text that is not supplied in the source.
laws.chronology: Current but unresolved; no safe operative UCMJ text or later superseding repeal is determinable from the reviewed source set.
laws.repeal_transfer_redesignation: No repeal or transfer issue can be resolved because the referenced operative UCMJ text is absent.
laws.rationale: The enactment is a source defect: it points to an external proposed UCMJ that is not provided. The safe treatment is a narrow explanatory note, not invented permanent code text.

laws.title: Public Law 15-92 | Commerce and Labor Innovation Initiative Act
laws.final_statutory_text: Commerce-and-labor licensing and revocation framework for media and business licenses, but the later implementation materials treat it as superseded before codification.
laws.chronology: Superseded before codification by later enactment PL 31-226, so no current Code insertion is appropriate.
laws.repeal_transfer_redesignation: Later repeal/supersession eliminates any surviving transfer or redesignation issue.
laws.rationale: The later-law posture means there is no live positive-law target to place. Historical-note treatment is sufficient.
